outcome_text: Environmental impact and sustainability of AI model training and management activities—as identified in the MAP function—are assessed and documented.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI environmental impact assessment, including evaluating computational resource consumption during model training, assessing sustainability of AI management activities, measuring carbon footprint and energy usage, and documenting environmental impact findings.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI environmental impact assessment, including evaluating computational resource consumption during model training, assessing sustainability of AI management activities, measuring carbon footprint and energy usage, and documenting environmental impact findings.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.12 (MEASURE) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-004 is the strongest match (STS: 0.609, cosine: 0.595). Secondary: AG-S-005 (AG, cosine: 0.593), AG-T-006 (AG, cosine: 0.576).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.610). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessment, impact, knowledge]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [activities, assessing, carbon, computational, consumption, documenting] vs WIDAI terms [explainability, fairness, human, methodologies, methods, metrics] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.889 (P=0.888, R=0.891). Balanced P/R — the 31-word competency implication and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.595 ≈ STS 0.610 (gap 0.015). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, assessment, impact, knowledge] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 18.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessment, impact, knowledge]. NIST AI RMF-only: [activities, assessing, carbon, computational, consumption, documenting]. WIDAI-only: [explainability, fairness, human, methodologies, methods, metrics].
